title: Aster neobiennis
intro: Aster neobiennis, also known as the Wood's Aster, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Aster neobiennis is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Aster neobiennis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and salves to support skin health and wound healing.
taxonomy: Aster neobiensis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the order Asterales and the genus Aster. The species Aster neobiensis is further divided into several subspecies. Aster neobiensis is native to Eastern North America, where it grows in open woods and along streams. This flowering plant is also known as the wood aster or eastern wood aster.
